subject: Human Resources
title: HR vs. Personnel Management
description: A micro-lesson that makes HR vs. Personnel Management usable.

hook.text: Before HR existed, companies had 'personnel departments.' They handled paperwork—and that was about it.

buildup.text: Personnel management focused on administrative tasks: payroll, attendance, and record keeping.

discovery.text: HR evolved from personnel by adding strategy—workforce planning, culture, and talent development.

twist.text: The name change wasn't cosmetic. It reflected a shift from managing files to managing futures.

climax.text: Modern HR sits at the executive table, shaping decisions about growth, mergers, and organizational design.

punchline.text: Personnel kept records. HR shapes the business.

quiz.question: How does HR differ from old-style personnel management?
options: HR adds strategy and talent development
